fields.label: NCBI API Key
fields.placeholder: Optional - raises rate limit to 10 req/sec
fields.helpText: Register at ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account/ to get an API key. Without a key: 3 req/sec.

healthcheck_config.description: Validates PubMed E-utilities access via database info endpoint

metadata.summary: Access PubMed biomedical and life sciences literature from the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI).
metadata.setup_guide:
1. Visit ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account/ and sign in or register
2. Go to Settings > API Key Management
3. Click 'Create an API Key'
4. Copy the key (optional but recommended for higher rate limits)

llm_usage_hint.overview: PubMed E-utilities API. Base URL: https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils. If $PUBMED_API_KEY is set, append &api_key= to all requests. Main tools: esearch.fcgi (search), efetch.fcgi (retrieve), elink.fcgi (find related). Always include &retmode=json for JSON output (where supported).
gotchas: Without API key: 3 requests/sec. With key: 10 requests/sec. Include api_key parameter or NCBI blocks.
esearch returns PMIDs (numeric IDs), not paper content. Use efetch with the IDs to get abstracts/metadata.
efetch for pubmed returns XML, not JSON (retmode=json is not supported for efetch). Parse <PubmedArticle> elements.
MeSH terms in square brackets filter by Medical Subject Headings: [MeSH], [MeSH Major Topic], [Author], [Journal], etc.
Use usehistory=y with esearch to store results on the server, then retrieve with WebEnv and query_key -- avoids passing huge ID lists.
PMC (PubMed Central) has full-text articles via a separate API. PubMed itself only has abstracts.
